summary: Exact-match decompilation work for GM/game-mode files, with small ground header/source edits. The PR body claims 7 decompiled files and 9 newly matched functions; changed-file metadata lists 9 touched files including ground.c and ground.h. Review focused on avoiding data-order externs, avoiding expanded JObj assert code, replacing raw u8 pointer math with typed struct fields, and restoring a likely source for-loop shape.

key_files.role: GM decomp/matching edits including gm_8016247C, gm_80164910, gm_80166378, fn_8016719C, and gm_8016A164 diff excerpts.
evidence_refs: text_corpus bot report: gm_1601 new matches/improvements

key_files.role: GM training/classic-style matching edits; review flagged data-order extern and expanded JObj assert regression.
GM matching edit around fn_801981A0; review required typed state-field access instead of raw pointer offsets.
GM matching edits listed in PR body and changed-file metadata; detailed diff not loaded in excerpt.
evidence_refs: text_corpus bot report: fn_801A94BC new match and gm_801A7B00 improvement

key_files.role: GM match-exit/result matching; review required avoiding new u8 aliases and using MatchExitInfo fields.
GM regular-clear matching; review required likely for-loop form with ordered initialization in the for statement.
GM result matching edits with several bot-reported matches/improvements; detailed diff not loaded in excerpt.
evidence_refs: text_corpus bot report: fn_80175D34 and fn_80174284 new matches; fn_80175DC8, fn_80176D3C, fn_80174B4C improvements

key_files.role: Small ground source edit listed only in changed-file metadata.
Small ground header edit listed only in changed-file metadata.

what_changed.text: PR body describes exact-match decompilation of 7 GM files and 9 newly matched functions, verified with ninja changes_all and clean regression reports.
Changed-file metadata lists 9 touched files: seven src/melee/gm files plus src/melee/gr/ground.c and ground.h.
Bot report for commit range 35ad0ea-b6b9840 reports +0.15% matched code, +5852 bytes, 8 new matches, and 15 improvements in unmatched items.

gm_1601.c diff excerpt shows cleanup/reshaping of locals and direct indexed player_standings field assignments in gm_80166378, plus a local bitfield struct in fn_8016719C and ternary facing-direction assignment.
gm_1832.c diff excerpt shows local lifetime/declaration changes, switch case reshaping, repeated lbl_804735E8.xE4 reads captured as kind, and JObj translation/scale operations.
Review follow-up states gm_18A5.c raw offsets were replaced by typed state accesses and gm_18A5.o remained byte-identical to the previous build.
Review follow-up states gm_1BA8.c dropped a u8 alias and used MatchExitInfo fields in gm_804979D8; linked main.dol was still byte-identical to retail.
Review follow-up states gmregclear.c was converted back to a for loop with ordered initialization in the for statement and gmregclear.o remained byte-identical to the previous build.

smart_moves.text: Reviewer-requested typed field recovery in gm_18A5.c preserved the match while removing raw u8-offset indexing.
Reviewer-requested MatchExitInfo field access in gm_1BA8.c clarified the true containing object gm_804979D8 and avoided a misleading alias to gm_80497758.
The loop in gmregclear.c was restored to a for-loop source shape while keeping initialization order in the for statement.
The PR includes narrow verification claims: slice applied alone against baseline 0b15e71345 with ninja changes_all and clean regression report, plus full ship-set verification.

decomp_lessons.lesson: Do not add extern constants solely to make a function match when the effect is just data ordering.

Expanded JObj assert-like code should be mapped back to an appropriate HSD_JObj helper when the operation has a known helper shape; in this PR, review suggested HSD_JObjGetTranslation2 for a single-assert case.
Avoid introducing new u8 pointer aliases for known state or result data; prefer typed state structs and named fields where the offsets are understood.
When a loop shape looks likely original, keep it as a for loop and place order-sensitive initialization in the for initializer instead of switching to a do-while solely for matching.

naming_conventions.text: Reviewer-accepted follow-up names/accesses for gm_18A5.c used `state` and field paths such as `state->x2A[pnum].state`, `state->x21[pnum]`, and `state->x44[pnum]` rather than `state_ptr[pnum + offset]`.
Reviewer-accepted follow-up for gm_1BA8.c used `MatchExitInfo`-style fields including `match_end`, `result`, `frame_count`, and `player_standings[0].stocks` / `.percent` for resolved offsets.
Diff excerpt shows local role naming such as `internal_id`, `unlock_idx`, `notify_val`, `kind`, and `team_i`; evidence supports these as local PR names but not as global naming rules.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: PR body claims runner-validated exact matches only, with isolation verification against baseline 0b15e71345 using ninja changes_all and clean regression report.
Author follow-up comments record byte-identical object or DOL checks after review cleanups in gm_18A5.c, gm_1BA8.c, and gmregclear.c.
Diff excerpt includes PAD_STACK usage in gm_1601.c; no review evidence in this slice explains or validates that tactic beyond the overall PR verification claims.

Diff excerpt shows pragma churn near fn_801891F4 (`#pragma dont_inline on` to `off`); review feedback instead focused on avoiding expanded JObj assert code, so the pragma should not be generalized from this slice.

review_feedback.feedback: Using an extern to make a function match due to data ordering was called a regression.
review_feedback.author: PsiLupan

review_feedback.feedback: Expanded NULL/assert code around a JObj should instead be a call to something like HSD_JObjGetTranslation2 when there is only a single assert.
review_feedback.author: PsiLupan

review_feedback.feedback: Pointer math over state data was called a regression; reviewer wanted the field at offset 0x14 of the pointer indexed at pnum.
review_feedback.author: jurrejelle

review_feedback.feedback: Do not introduce new u8 pointers where struct accesses are possible.
review_feedback.author: jurrejelle

review_feedback.feedback: Likely source should be a for loop; if initialization order matters, put it in the for-loop initializer.
review_feedback.author: jurrejelle

review_feedback.feedback: Author reported fixing raw offsets with typed state accesses while preserving byte-identical gm_18A5.o.
review_feedback.author: fjooord

review_feedback.feedback: Author reported resolving offsets inside gm_804979D8 MatchExitInfo and replacing casts with proper field accesses while keeping linked main.dol byte-identical.
review_feedback.author: fjooord

review_feedback.feedback: Author reported converting back to a for loop with ordered initialization and byte-identical gmregclear.o.
review_feedback.author: fjooord

follow_up_queries: PR2656 gm_1832 extern lbl_804DA60C data ordering regression
PR2656 gm_18A5 fn_801981A0 state->x2A state->x21 state->x44
PR2656 gmregclear fn_80181C80 for loop initializer

notes: PR identity, author, merge time, changed-file list, PR body, and review comments are directly loaded.
Raw diff is truncated, so detailed claims are limited to visible hunks and review-comment hunks.
PR body claims 9 newly matched functions, while loaded bot report excerpt lists 8 new matches and 15 improvements; this record preserves the discrepancy instead of resolving it.
ground.c and ground.h are present in changed-file metadata but not in the PR body's file list; no detailed diff hunk for those files is loaded.
Author follow-up comments provide byte-identical object/DOL claims for specific fixes; no independent build logs beyond PR body and bot report are loaded here.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Avoid externs that only satisfy data ordering in code-match PRs
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2656, review called adding `extern const f32 lbl_804DA60C;` in gm_1832.c a regression because it made a function match due to data ordering. This supports preferring real data ownership/split fixes or leaving data work out of focused code-match PRs.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Prefer typed field access over raw u8 offsets when offsets are understood
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2656, reviewers rejected new raw u8 pointer math in gm_18A5.c and gm_1BA8.c. Follow-up comments say the code was changed to typed state and MatchExitInfo field accesses while preserving object/DOL identity.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use for-loop initializer ordering before replacing a likely source for loop
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2656, gmregclear.c review identified fn_80181C80 as most likely a for loop and suggested putting required initialization order in the for statement. The author reported converting back to `for (var_r29 = 1, var_r30 = 0, sp10 = data->xC; var_r29 < 6; var_r29++)` with byte-identical output.

source_update_candidates.title: Add concrete data-order extern example
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding PR #2656 as an example under data/literal ownership standards: adding an extern such as `extern const f32 lbl_804DA60C;` only to make a function match was reviewed as a regression caused by data ordering.
source_update_candidates.reason: The loaded standards already cover the principle; this PR provides a concrete reviewer-backed example.

source_update_candidates.title: Add JObj single-assert helper example
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding PR #2656 as an example under header-inlines/assert cleanup: expanded JObj NULL/assert code in fn_801891F4 was reviewed as a regression and should be a helper call such as `HSD_JObjGetTranslation2` when the evidence indicates a single assert.
source_update_candidates.reason: The standard already says to map expanded header asserts back to helpers; this PR supplies a named helper example for curator review.

source_update_candidates.title: GM match-exit offsets in gm_1BA8.c resolved to gm_804979D8 MatchExitInfo in this PR
source_update_candidates.text: For PR #2656 curator review only: author follow-up says offsets in gm_801BB758 resolved inside `gm_804979D8` as a `MatchExitInfo`, not `gm_80497758`, and became field accesses such as `match_end.result`, `frame_count`, and `player_standings[0].stocks`.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is a possible path-specific fact, but should be checked against current source before promotion.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the PR-wide count of newly matched functions as a single settled value without reconciling PR body 9 vs bot report 8.
Do not infer detailed ground.c or ground.h changes from this slice; only changed-file metadata is loaded.
Do not generalize PAD_STACK or pragma changes as accepted tactics from this PR; the slice lacks narrow review justification for those tactics beyond overall verification.
Do not treat all local variable renames in the diff as naming standards; only review-backed typed field/access cleanup is strong.
